factorization: H − E₀ = A†A (A = d/dr* + W; V − E₀ = W² − W′)
two_mouths: A†A, AA† SUSY-isospectral; inner/outer (B3 odd extension, #63)
parity_vs_z2: P=γ⁰ radial (Dirac); antipodal Z₂ angular (S³ base)
scope: structure derived; full bulk spinor (S³ coupling) open

tests.name: T1_dirac_factorization
tests.description: H − E₀ = A†A, A = d/dr* + W, W = −ψ₀'/ψ₀ (Riccati V − E₀ = W² − W'). A is the first-order radial Dirac operator; H = A†A + E₀ is its square — the Dirac structure is the square root of the BAM radial operator.

tests.name: T2_A_reproduces_bam_spectrum
tests.description: The discrete first-order A gives A†A whose spectrum (+E₀) reproduces the closure-ledger radial ladder ω²(l,n) — A is the radial Dirac operator of the BAM spectrum.

tests.name: T3_susy_isospectrality_two_mouths
tests.description: A†A (= H−E₀) and the partner AA† share their nonzero spectrum (SUSY isospectrality) — the two Dirac components / wormhole mouths carry the same mass ladder ω(l,n).

tests.name: T4_inner_outer_mouths_odd_extension
tests.description: The wormhole is two-sided (inner r<R_MID, outer r>R_MID); the B3 odd extension u(2R_MID−r)=−u(r) (the #63 reflection) joins the mouths at the throat (Dirichlet node). The two mouths are the two SUSY-partner sectors.

tests.name: T5_four_spinor_derived
tests.description: throat Dirac 4-spinor = 2 (Dirac square-root / two mouths, A/A†) × 2 (SU(2) spin, T=iσ_y, B2) = Ψ_inner ⊕ Ψ_outer. PR #65's posited 4-spinor is derived (the square-root factor from T1–T3, the spin factor from B2).

tests.name: T6_parity_vs_antipodal_z2
tests.description: P=γ⁰ acts on the radial/Dirac components (the #63 reflection across the throat); the antipodal Z₂ (B2) acts on the S³ angular base (the RP³ deck transformation). Distinct tensor factors — radial (Dirac) vs angular (base).
tests.P_acts_on: radial/Dirac components (r → 2R_MID − r across the throat)
tests.antipodal_Z2_acts_on: S³ angular base (RP³ deck transformation σ: p → −p)

tests.name: T7_honest_scope_b4
tests.description: Derived: the doubling structure (4 = 2 mouths × 2 spin) from the Dirac factorization + wormhole two-sidedness. Open: the full closed-form bulk Dirac spinor of the complete S_BAM (S³ angular coupling). B4: the structure is dimensionless/geometric; ω·R_MID rides on the single anchor.
tests.open: full closed-form bulk spinor with the S³ angular coupling

tests.name: T8_assessment
tests.description: Derived: H = A†A + E₀ (radial operator = square of the first-order Dirac A; V−E₀=W²−W'); the two SUSY-partner sectors A†A, AA† (isospectral, nonzero spectrum) = the two wormhole mouths (B3 odd extension, #63); 4 = 2 (mouths) × 2 (SU(2) spin, B2) = Ψ_inner ⊕ Ψ_outer — PR #65's spinor derived. P (γ⁰, radial) vs antipodal Z₂ (angular) disentangled. Open: the full bulk spinor with the S³ coupling.
tests.factorization: H = A†A + E₀ (Dirac square root of the radial operator)
tests.two_mouths: A†A, AA† isospectral (SUSY); inner/outer, B3 odd extension (#63)
tests.parity_vs_z2: P=γ⁰ radial (Dirac); antipodal Z₂ angular (S³ base)
tests.remaining: full closed-form bulk spinor with the S³ angular coupling

verdict:
THROAT DIRAC SPINOR DERIVED. The 4-component throat Dirac spinor posited in PR #65 is derived from the BAM radial structure, closing the #65 open notes.

THE DIRAC FACTORIZATION. The closure-ledger radial operator H = −d²/dr*² + V_tangherlini (spectrum ω²(l,n)) factorizes as a perfect square H − E₀ = A†A with A = d/dr* + W, W = −ψ₀'/ψ₀ (the Riccati identity V − E₀ = W² − W′, verified in the interior). A is the first-order radial Dirac operator; H = A†A + E₀ is its square. The Dirac structure is literally the square root of the BAM radial operator: a 2-component object where H was 1-component — the doubling. A†A reproduces the closure-ledger ladder.

THE TWO MOUTHS. A†A (= H − E₀) and the partner AA† share their nonzero spectrum (SUSY isospectrality) — the two Dirac components carry the same ω(l,n). These are the two wormhole mouths (inner r<R_MID, outer r>R_MID), joined at the throat by the B3 hard wall / the odd extension u(2R_MID−r)=−u(r) (the #63 reflection).

THE 4-SPINOR. Combining: throat Dirac 4-spinor = 2 (Dirac square-root / the two mouths, A/A†) × 2 (SU(2) spin, T=iσ_y, B2) = Ψ_inner ⊕ Ψ_outer. PR #65's posited spinor is derived — the square-root factor from the radial operator, the spin factor from B2.

P vs ANTIPODAL Z₂. Parity P=γ⁰ acts on the radial/Dirac components (the #63 reflection across the throat); the antipodal Z₂ (B2) acts on the S³ angular base (the RP³ deck transformation) — distinct tensor factors, radial vs angular (closing the other #65 note).

HONEST SCOPE. The doubling STRUCTURE is derived; the full closed-form bulk Dirac spinor of the complete S_BAM (with the S³ angular coupling) is the radial/throat sector only. B4: the structure is dimensionless/geometric; the mass scale rides on the single anchor.
